claim: Independent cross-check confirms YOLO demand evidence dispatch read-only tool restrictions are correctly implemented: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_CLAUDE_READ_ONLY_SETTINGS defines permissions.allow=[Read,Glob,Grep] and permissions.deny=[Write,Edit,MultiEdit,NotebookEdit,Bash,Agent,TaskCreate,TaskUpdate]; executionConfig applies these as ai.settings JSON string, ai.claude_tools, ai.claude_allowed_tools, ai.claude_disallowed_tools, and safety flags; buildProviderInvocation translates config into Claude CLI --allowedTools and --disallowedTools arguments.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_CLAUDE_READ_ONLY_SETTINGS constant defines permissions.allow array with Read, Glob, Grep and permissions.deny array with Write, Edit, MultiEdit, NotebookEdit, Bash, Agent, TaskCreate, TaskUpdate.
evidence.why: Primary source for read-only tool restriction schema.

evidence.summary: executionConfig function when provider===claude sets ai.settings to JSON.stringify(DEMAND_EVIDENCE_CLAUDE_READ_ONLY_SETTINGS), ai.claude_tools='Read,Glob,Grep', ai.claude_allowed_tools='Read,Glob,Grep', ai.claude_disallowed_tools='Write,Edit,MultiEdit,NotebookEdit,Bash,Agent,TaskCreate,TaskUpdate', ai.claude_disable_slash_commands=true, ai.claude_no_session_persistence=true.
evidence.why: Verifies read-only settings are applied to Claude provider config.

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation for claude provider constructs args array with --tools from ai.claude_tools, --allowedTools from ai.claude_allowed_tools, and --disallowedTools from ai.claude_disallowed_tools after cleaning with cleanString.
evidence.why: Verifies provider adapter translates config to Claude CLI arguments.

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation conditionally adds --disable-slash-commands when ai.claude_disable_slash_commands===true and --no-session-persistence when ai.claude_no_session_persistence===true.
evidence.why: Verifies additional Claude safety flags are translated to CLI arguments.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines explorer, cross_checker, and verifier protocols with writes_code:false and result_schema:'yolo.demand.evidence_result.v1'.
evidence.why: Verifies evidence agent protocols specify read-only constraint at contract level.

evidence.summary: executionConfig loads config, extracts provider/model options, applies codex read-only sandbox/approval when provider===codex, applies Claude read-only settings when provider===claude, applies custom read-only sandbox when provider===custom, returns merged config.
evidence.why: Verifies execution config centralizes provider-specific read-only constraints.

assumptions: Claude CLI respects --allowedTools and --disallowedTools flags and actually prevents denied tools from being used at runtime.
The permissions schema in DEMAND_EVIDENCE_CLAUDE_READ_ONLY_SETTINGS is valid input for Claude CLI --settings flag.
No code path between executionConfig and buildProviderInvocation modifies ai.claude_* fields or overrides the settings.
Agent provider dispatch does not bypass provider-adapter when spawning evidence agents.
risks: If Claude CLI does not enforce --allowedTools/--disallowedTools at runtime, read-only protection fails despite correct configuration.
If executionConfig is bypassed or config.ai is mutated after calling executionConfig, read-only constraints may not apply.
If provider adapter is bypassed in some dispatch paths, Claude CLI arguments may not be applied.
The --permission-mode default in provider-adapter could allow permission changes that affect tool availability beyond allowedTools/disallowedTools.
missing: No integration tests found that execute provider-adapter with real Claude CLI to verify --allowedTools and --disallowedTools behavior.
No tests found that verify evidence agents actually cannot use Write, Edit, Bash, or other denied tools at runtime.
No documentation found on Claude CLI --allowedTools/--disallowedTools enforcement semantics.
No tests found comparing codex provider read-only sandbox behavior with Claude provider read-only tool restrictions.

result.notes: Independent code reads confirm explorer's claim: source code correctly implements DEMAND_EVIDENCE_CLAUDE_READ_ONLY_SETTINGS schema, executionConfig applies it to Claude provider config, and buildProviderInvocation translates config to Claude CLI arguments. No conflicts found with explorer evidence. The claim is source-verified but requires runtime verification (missing integration tests) to treat as runtime-guaranteed fact.
